Esther
Esther
Esther
Est.
Esther
Introduction

This account tells about the life of a young Jewish woman named Esther. These events happened back when King Ahasuerus (more widely known as King Xerxes) ruled over the kingdom of Persia. After Queen Vashti was banished for refusing to dance in front the kingвҖҷs guests at a drinking party, Esther was chosen as the new queen without revealing her Jewish background. But there was a high official called Haman who was offended by EstherвҖҷs Jewish guardian and in his anger, determined to annihilate all of the Jews from the kingdom. This account reveals how EtherвҖҷs courage and her love for her Jewish people leads her to help them and eventually helps to save their lives.
The account also reveals the source of a Jewish feast named вҖҳPurimвҖҷ, which to this day is a time when Jewish people celebrate how God saved them from their enemy, Haman.
Main components of this account
Esther becomes Queen of Persia
HamanвҖҷs threat to the Jews
HamanвҖҷs execution
The Jewish people destroy their enemies
This is still a very early look into the unfinished text of the Open English Translation of the Bible. Please double-check the text in advance before using in public.
Queen Vashti refuses King Ahasuerus
/s1 King AhasuerusвҖҷs banquet; Queen Vashti Deposed; Queen Vashti Defies King Xerxes; King Ahasuerus Deposes Queen Vashti; The KingвҖҷs Banquet
The following events happened during the reignВҰ228506 of
King
AhasuerusВҰ228503.
Ezr 4:6.
AlsoВҰ228501 known as King Xerxes,
he ruled
the empire of Persia
withВҰ228502 127 provincesВҰ228514 from IndiaВҰ228507 all the way across to EthiopiaВҰ228510.)
In thoseВҰ228517 daysВҰ228516, he ruled his empire from
a city called
ShushanВҰ228526,
Known as вҖҳSusaвҖҷ in many 20th century translations, even though earlier English translations often got it correct.
PersiaвҖҷs
capital.
In the third year of his reignВҰ228531, he hosted a celebration for all of his officials and for every important person who worked for him, as well as the officers who served in the army of the
combined empire
of PersiaВҰ228540 and MediaВҰ228541, the wealthy landowners, and the officials of the provincesВҰ228544. The king was presentВҰ228545 in person
to host the celebration
which, over a period of six months, would displayВҰ228547 the incredible wealth and prestige of his empire and his personal wealth and power.
ThenВҰ228564 atВҰ228564 the end of that time, the kingВҰ228569 hosted
another

celebration, butВҰ228564 this time for all the peopleВҰ228572 in the royal fort in ShushanВҰ228574, from the nobles to the least prestigious. He held this celebration over an entire week in the enclosed gardenВҰ228584 of the royal palaceВҰ228585.

There were white and blue curtains hanging from white and purpleВҰ228596 cordsВҰ228593 attached to silver rings on marble pillarsВҰ228601. The guests reclined on goldВҰ228605 and silver couchesВҰ228603 set on a mosaic floor that was made of red and white marble, and mother-of-pearlВҰ228612 and other expensive stonesВҰ228613.
Drinks were servedВҰ228615 inВҰ228616 goldenВҰ228617 goblets, andВҰ228621 each one was a unique design. The kingвҖҷsВҰ228625 wine seemed limitless as he displayed his generosity,
andВҰ228646 it was served accordingВҰ228628 to his rule: ThereВҰ228631 is no compulsion to drinkВҰ228627, butВҰ228627 all of his servants were to give each guest however much they wanted.
Meanwhile, Queen VashtiВҰ228649 was hosting a celebration for the womenВҰ228653
in a different part
of the kingвҖҷs royalВҰ228655 palace.
/s1 Queen Vashti Deposed

On the seventh dayВҰ228661 of the celebrations, when the king was feeling cheery from his wine-drinking, he ordered seven of his chief servants (MehumanВҰ228669, BizthaВҰ228670, HarbonaВҰ228671, BigthaВҰ228672, AbagthaВҰ228673, ZetharВҰ228674, and Karkas)

to bringВҰ228685 Queen VashtiВҰ228688, wearing her royalВҰ228693 crownВҰ228692, to him inВҰ228692 orderВҰ228694 to show her beautyВҰ228699 to the peopleВҰ228695 and the officialsВҰ228696, becauseВҰ228700 sheВҰ228704 was very attractive.
ButВҰ228706 when the servants came and told her the kingвҖҷs commandВҰ228710, Queen VashtiВҰ228708 refusedВҰ228706 to come. WhenВҰ228706 the king found this out, he got very angryВҰ228715 and
sat there
raging.
SoВҰ228724 he
called over
his advisors who knew the law and could make good decisions, and discussed with them what he should do.
(His closestВҰ228739 advisors were Karshena, ShetharВҰ228743, AdmathaВҰ228744, TarshishВҰ228745, MeresВҰ228746, MarsenaВҰ228747, and Memukan. These sevenВҰ228749 officials were from
various places in the empire
of PersiaВҰ228752 and MediaВҰ228753 and they advised the kingВҰ228756 personally,
thus making them
the most powerful officials in the empire.)
вҖңAccordingВҰ228761 to
our
law,вҖқ
the kingВҰ228776 asked,
вҖңwhat should be done withВҰ228765 QueenВҰ228773 VashtiВҰ228766, since sheВҰ228772 refused to obey my commandВҰ228775 when the servants passed it onВҰ228765 to her?вҖқ
Then Memukan answered the king,
speaking loudly enough that

both the king and his officials could hear. вҖңQueen VashtiВҰ228794 has done wrong,вҖқ he said, вҖңand not only against the king. She has also done wrong against all the officials and peopleВҰ228806 groups in all the provincesВҰ228810 that KingВҰ228786 AhasuerusВҰ228812
rules
Soon

women all over the empire will hear about what the queenВҰ228819 did and theyвҖҷll say, вҖҳKingВҰ228829 AhasuerusВҰ228830 commanded hisВҰ228837 servants to bringВҰ228832 Queen VashtiВҰ228835 to him, butВҰ228838 she didnвҖҷtВҰ228838 go! Then women will stop respecting theirВҰ228826 husbandsВҰ228826.
Even today, the leading women of PersiaВҰ228847 and MediaВҰ228849 will hear what the queenВҰ228855 did and start doing the same to
their husbands who are
officialsВҰ228857, and this will cause
a wave of
contemptВҰ228860 and angerВҰ228861.
If it pleases you
to do so
your majesty

, you should personally issue a royal decree and have it added to the laws of PersiaВҰ228876 and MediaВҰ228878 which cannot be repealed. This decree should say that VashtiВҰ228885 can never again comeВҰ228884 into your presence, and then you should choose a different womanВҰ228892 for the royal positionВҰ228889вҖ”someone whoвҖҷs betterВҰ228893 than herВҰ228889.

YourВҰ228905 decreeВҰ228897 will be proclaimedВҰ228896 right through your empire, even though itвҖҷs so hugeВҰ228906, andВҰ228896 thenВҰ228896 all womenВҰ228910, irrespective of their status or position, will honourВҰ228912 their husbandsВҰ228913.
This seemed like a good idea to the king andВҰ228919 his officialsВҰ228923, so KingВҰ228922 Ahasuerus followed the adviceВҰ228920 of Memukan

and sent letters to every province in his empire. He wrote to every province using its own alphabet and to each peopleВҰ228944 group in its own language. The letters said that men should be the masters over their wives and children, and that a husband should be able to give orders to his wife in his own native language
that she should understand and obey
Beautiful Esther enters the kingвҖҷs harem
/s1 Esther becomes queen; Esther Becomes Queen; Esther Made Queen
Some time later when KingВҰ228963 AhasuerusВҰ228964 no longer felt so angry, he
started to miss
VashtiВҰ228968. ButВҰ228969 when sheвҖҷd disobeyed his command, he had made a decree
thatВҰ228970 she could never come into his presence againВҰ228977
SoВҰ228979 some of the young men who served the king suggested to him, вҖң
Your majesty, you shouldВҰ228984 get a new wife for yourself.
You could tell your servants to look for young virginsВҰ228987 who are very beautiful.

You could assign officers in each provinceВҰ228996 of your empire to bring everyВҰ229001 virgin who is very beautiful here to your capital city of ShushanВҰ229010. They could stay in the harem for virgins under the custody of HegaiВҰ229019, the castrated royal guardian who takes care of the young women who live there andВҰ228991 he could arrange for them to receive beauty treatmentsВҰ229025.
ThenВҰ229027 you could decide which youngВҰ229027 womanВҰ229027 you like best andВҰ229027 thenВҰ229027 make her queen instead of VashtiВҰ229034.вҖқ
The king liked what they suggestedВҰ229036, so he did it.

At that time, there was a Jewish man named Mordekai living inВҰ229046 the capital city of ShushanВҰ229046. (He was from the tribe of Benjamin: his father was Jair, his grandfather was ShimeiВҰ229054, and his great-grandfather was KishВҰ229057
whoвҖҷd been taken away from Jerusalem and led to Babylon when KingВҰ229071 NebuchadnezzerВҰ229076 of Babylon had exiledВҰ229066 JeconiahВҰ229070, the king of Judah, along with many of his people.)
Now Mordekai was taking care of his cousin, who was an orphan. WhenВҰ229080 her fatherВҰ229093 and mother had died, Mordekai had adoptedВҰ229104 her as his own daughterВҰ229087. HerВҰ229102
Hebrew
name was HadassahВҰ229084, and her
Persian
name was Esther. Esther was now a youngВҰ229095 womanВҰ229095 and she was exceptionally attractive.

SoВҰ229109 when the kingвҖҷs command and decree became known, many youngВҰ229116 women were being brought into the capital city of ShushanВҰ229120 and placed under HegaiвҖҷs charge, and this is what happened to EstherВҰ229127. (Hegai was the overseer of the women.)

Hegai was very impressed withВҰ229141 Esther, and he treated her favourablyВҰ229143. He quicklyВҰ229145 arranged for Esther to receive her beautyВҰ229148 treatments and her allotment of food, as well as choosing sevenВҰ229155 female servants from the kingвҖҷs palaceВҰ229161 and assigningВҰ229158 them to be her personal attendants. He also movedВҰ229163 Esther and her attendants to the best rooms inВҰ229141 the harem for virgins.
(EstherВҰ229174 hadnвҖҷt revealed her family ties or her Jewishness to anyone, becauseВҰ229181 Mordekai had instructedВҰ229183 her not to.)
Every day Mordekai would walkВҰ229195 around inВҰ229209 frontВҰ229196 of the courtyardВҰ229197 of the womenвҖҷsВҰ229200 harem to find out
from others
how EstherВҰ229205 was doing andВҰ229190 how she was being treated.
Each youngВҰ229213 womanВҰ229213 would get twelve months of beautyВҰ229233 treatmentВҰ229233 accordingВҰ229224 to the
Persian
law for womenвҖ”there would be six months of applying oilВҰ229236 of myrrhВҰ229237, thenВҰ229211 six months of perfumesВҰ229240 andВҰ229211 other lotions
designed for
women. ThenВҰ229211 theirВҰ229233 turn would come, one atВҰ229211 a timeВҰ229232, to be taken to KingВҰ229219 AhasuerusВҰ229220.
TheВҰ229250 youngВҰ229245 womanВҰ229245 was allowed to takeВҰ229257 whatever she wanted from the haremВҰ229259 when she went to the kingвҖҷsВҰ229264 houseВҰ229263.

SheвҖҷd be taken across in the eveningВҰ229266, and in the morningВҰ229270 sheвҖҷd be led out to the other harem which was for the concubinesВҰ229285. (That harem was overseen by ShaashgazВҰ229281, another castrated royal guardian.) The womenВҰ229276 taken there wouldnвҖҷt go againВҰ229289 to the kingвҖҷs houseВҰ229275 unless he had especially liked them and summonedВҰ229299 her back by nameВҰ229300.
Esther becomes queen

Now Esther was the daughter of MordekaiвҖҷs uncleВҰ229309, AbihailВҰ229308, from whom Mordekai had adopted her as his own daughter. WhenВҰ229302 it was her turn to go to the king, she didnвҖҷtВҰ229320 ask for anythingВҰ229322 other than what HegaiВҰ229329, the kingвҖҷs trusted official inВҰ229302 charge of the harem, had advised, andВҰ229302 everyoneВҰ229333 who saw Esther liked her.
SoВҰ229344 EstherВҰ229345 was takenВҰ229344 to KingВҰ229348 AhasuerusВҰ229349 atВҰ229354 his royal palace in JanuaryВҰ229359, in the seventhВҰ229362 yearВҰ229360 of his reignВҰ229363,
and the kingВҰ229366 lovedВҰ229365 EstherВҰ229369 more than any of the other women. He treated her more kindly and more favourablyВҰ229375 than any of the other young women
who had become his concubines
. He put a royalВҰ229384 crownВҰ229382 onВҰ229385 her headВҰ229385 and made her the
new
queenВҰ229386 to replace VashtiВҰ229388.
ThenВҰ229390 the kingВҰ229391 hosted a largeВҰ229393 celebration and invited all his officialsВҰ229396 and servantsВҰ229397 to a feast to celebrate EstherВҰ229400
becoming queen
. He proclaimed that this would be a time to celebrate for people in all of the provincesВҰ229402 of his empire when they wouldnвҖҷt have to pay taxes, and he gaveВҰ229404
many
generous gifts.
Mordekai and Esther earn the kingвҖҷs favour
/s1 Mordecai Discovers a Plot; Mordecai and Haman; Mordecai Saves the KingвҖҷs Life; Mordecai Uncovers a Conspiracy
Later onВҰ229409,
KingВҰ229416 Ahasuerus had
his officers
bring more virginsВҰ229410
to Shushan
. During this time, Mordekai
had been given a position by the king so
he sat
there
atВҰ229409 the kingвҖҷsВҰ229416 gateВҰ229414.
EstherВҰ229419 had still not toldВҰ229426 anyone that
she was Jewish
because Mordekai had warned herВҰ229421 not to tell anyone.
WhatвҖҷs more,
she continued to follow all of MordekaiвҖҷs instructionsВҰ229426, just as she had done when she was still under his guardianshipВҰ229437.
/s1 MordekaiвҖҷs Loyalty to the King
One day when Mordekai was
onВҰ229445 duty
atВҰ229445 the kingвҖҷs gateВҰ229445, two of the kingвҖҷs guardsВҰ229455 who protected the doorway
to the kingвҖҷs private rooms
became angryВҰ229448 withВҰ229445 the king and so they planned to assassinate KingВҰ229447 AhasuerusВҰ229461. (Their names were BigthanВҰ229449 and TereshВҰ229450.)
ButВҰ229463 when Mordekai heard what they were planning, he told Queen EstherВҰ229467 about it andВҰ229469 she told the kingВҰ229471, explaining that Mordekai had given her the information.
WhenВҰ229475 the matterВҰ229485 was investigatedВҰ229475 and
the facts
discovered, both conspirators were impaled onВҰ229484
sharpened wooden
poles, and in the presence of the kingВҰ229488, the incident was recorded in the royal record of events.
Haman plans to annihilate the Jews
/s1 HamanвҖҷs Plot against the Jews; Haman Plots to Destroy the Jews; Haman Undertakes to Destroy the Jews; HamanвҖҷs Plot to Destroy the Jews
Some time later, KingВҰ229496 AhasuerusВҰ229497 promoted Haman, one of his officials (and the son of HammedathaВҰ229503
who was a descendant
of Agag). The kingВҰ229496 gave Haman a very important positionВҰ229509вҖ”more important than any of his other officials.

ThenВҰ229517 the king commandedВҰ229531 that all his other officials had to bow down inВҰ229523 front of Haman to honour him, andВҰ229517 they were doing this atВҰ229523 the kingвҖҷs gateВҰ229523, except for Mordekai who refused to bow down to reverence Haman.
вҖңWhyВҰ229552 are youВҰ229550 disobeying the kingвҖҷs commandВҰ229553?вҖқ asked the other royal servantsВҰ229542 atВҰ229546 the kingвҖҷs gateВҰ229546.
Mordekai told them that it was becauseВҰ229571 he was a Jew
(andВҰ229556 Jews only worship Yahweh)
. He didnвҖҷtВҰ229562 listenВҰ229563 to their
warnings
so they told HamanВҰ229566 about it to seeВҰ229567 if he would tolerateВҰ229568 Mordekai inВҰ229557 keeping refusing to bow down.
WhenВҰ229580 Haman sawВҰ229580 that Mordekai would not bow down to him or show him reverence, heВҰ229589 was furious.
ButВҰ229593 Haman despisedВҰ229593 the idea of just punishing Mordekai because heвҖҷd been told that Mordekai
didnвҖҷt bow to him because
Mordekai was a Jew. SoВҰ229593 HamanВҰ229609 decided to annihilateВҰ229610 all of MordekaiвҖҷs people, i.e., all the Jews throughout the kingdomВҰ229619 of AhasuerusВҰ229620.
In early April of the twelfth year of KingВҰ229633 AhasuerusвҖҷ reign, HamanВҰ229640 got his servants to throwВҰ229635 dice
to select the best month and the best day to kill the Jews
, and the 13th of March was chosen.
Then HamanВҰ229656
went to
KingВҰ229657 AhasuerusВҰ229658 and said, вҖң
Your Majesty,

thereВҰ229659 is a certain group of people who live amongВҰ229665 the other peoples in every provinceВҰ229668 of your empire. They have theirВҰ229670 own set of laws, so they donвҖҷtВҰ229682 obey your laws. ItвҖҷs not good for you to allow them to continue to live in your empire.
Your majesty, if you approve of this plan, then write a decree saying that all of the Jews mustВҰ229700 be destroyedВҰ229694.
When theyвҖҷre dead, we can take all of theirВҰ229694 possessions, andВҰ229695 from that
I will give 300 tons of silverВҰ229699 to your administrators for them to put into your royal treasuriesВҰ229709.вҖқ
This seemed sensible to
the king so he removedВҰ229712 his signet ring from his finger andВҰ229712 he gaveВҰ229719 it
Having the kingвҖҷs signet ring enabled Haman to stamp the kingвҖҷs seal (like a signature) onto the newly drafted laws.
to Haman (the son of HammedathaВҰ229723, the AgagiteВҰ229724, the adversary of the Jews).
The kingВҰ229729 told HamanВҰ229730, вҖңYou can keep the moneyВҰ229731 for yourself, andВҰ229728 you can do whatever you want to do withВҰ229738 those peopleВҰ229734.вҖқ

On the thirteenth dayВҰ229747 of that same monthВҰ229743, Haman called in the royal scribes, and he dictated a letter to them. He toldВҰ229754 them to send copies to the royal officialsВҰ229771, the governorsВҰ229762 of each province, and the leaders of each people group
within the empire

. The scribes translated the letter so it could be sent to each province using its own alphabet and to each people group in its own languageВҰ229779. To show that he was sending the letter under the kingвҖҷs own authority, Haman sealedВҰ229784 each copy of the letter withВҰ229743 the ring that had the kingвҖҷs official seal onВҰ229743 it.

Couriers delivered the letters to the officials in every provinceВҰ229796 in the empire. The letters said to completely destroyВҰ229798 all the Jews, includingВҰ229800 the children andВҰ229788 womenВҰ229811, onВҰ229790 a single day. That was to be the thirteenth day of March early in the following year. The letters also stated that those who killed the Jews could take everything that belonged to them.

The letter told the officials to displayВҰ229835 copies where everyone could see themвҖ”that way all the peopleВҰ229838 in every single provinceВҰ229833 would know that the king had commanded this, andВҰ229834 they could get readyВҰ229840 for when the dayВҰ229841 came.

So couriersВҰ229844 hurriedВҰ229846 off withВҰ229847 the letters as the king had commandedВҰ229847. A herald in the capital cityВҰ229857 of Shushan also proclaimed what the letters said. Then the king and HamanВҰ229854 relaxed and drank together, butВҰ229849 there was
total
confusionВҰ229859 in Shushan
about how all of this would work out
Mordekai asks the queen for help
/s1 Mordecai Asks for EstherвҖҷs Help; Esther Agrees to Help the Jews; Esther plans to rescue the Jews; Mordecai Requests EstherвҖҷs Help; Mordecai Persuades Esther to Help
When Mordekai found out about what had been doneВҰ229869, he tore his clothesВҰ229874 and dressed in sackclothВҰ229876 and
threw
ashesВҰ229877
over himself (as a sign of mourning or distress)
and wentВҰ229878 out into the cityВҰ229880 centre, wailing loudlyВҰ229883 and bitterlyВҰ229884.
ButВҰ229886 no one was wearing mourning garb was allowed inside the kingвҖҷs gate, so when Mordekai reached the gate, he had to stay just outside it.

In every provinceВҰ229904 of the empire, the letter that said to destroy the Jews was publicly displayed and when the Jews heard about itВҰ229921, they were incredibly upset. They skipped meals and wailed loudly, and many of them also wore sackclothВҰ229919 and threw ashesВҰ229920 on themselves and lay on the ground.
WhenВҰ229924
Queen
EstherвҖҷsВҰ229929 young female attendants cameВҰ229924 with her guardians andВҰ229924 told her
what was going on outside
, even the queenВҰ229934 became very scared. She sentВҰ229936 Mordekai some good clothesВҰ229938 to wear instead of the sackclothВҰ229943, butВҰ229924 he wouldnвҖҷt acceptВҰ229946 them.

The kingВҰ229952 had assigned some of the royal guardians to Esther personally, so Esther calledВҰ229948 for one of themвҖ”a man named HathakВҰ229950. She toldВҰ229956 him to go out and speak with Mordekai and find out what he was so distressed about.
SoВҰ229970 HathakВҰ229971 wentВҰ229970 out to
talk to
MordekaiВҰ229974 who was out in the plaza in frontВҰ229980 of the kingвҖҷsВҰ229983 gateВҰ229981.
Mordekai told him everything thatВҰ229998 Haman was planning to do andВҰ229985 even told him how much moneyВҰ229997 Haman had said the king would get for his treasuriesВҰ230004 if
the king commanded people
to killВҰ230009 all the Jews.

Mordekai also gaveВҰ230022 Hathak a copy of the letter that the announcers had read out loud inВҰ230020 ShushanВҰ230020, that said that people must kill all the Jews. He told Hathak to show the letter to EstherВҰ230027 so that she would know exactly what it said and also told him to urge her to go to the kingВҰ230035 personally and to beg him desperately to save her people
from destructionВҰ230021
SoВҰ230045 HathakВҰ230046 returned to the queen andВҰ230047 told her what Mordekai had said,
andВҰ230056 EstherВҰ230054 toldВҰ230056 himВҰ230056 to go back to Mordekai with this message:

вҖңThereвҖҷs a law about going to the king that applies to everyoneВҰ230071 in the kingdom, both men and women. If anyoneВҰ230089 goes into the innerВҰ230084 courtyardВҰ230083 of the palace, where the king can see them, and the king has not summoned them, that person will be executed. Only if the king holdsВҰ230094 out his goldenВҰ230101 scepterВҰ230100 to them, then they will live. (EveryoneВҰ230089 in the whole empire knows this law.) The king hasnвҖҷt called for me in over a month
, and if I go in without being summoned, I could be put to deathВҰ230091
.вҖқ
SoВҰ230117 Hathak
went back to Mordekai andВҰ230117 told him what the queen had said.
Mordekai told
Hathak
to tell this to EstherВҰ230129: вҖңDonвҖҷtВҰ230130 imagineВҰ230132 that just because you live there inВҰ230133 the kingвҖҷs palace that youвҖҷll be safe when they kill all the other Jews.

If you say nothing atВҰ230147 all now, someone from some other placeВҰ230153 will rescue the Jews, butВҰ230150 you and your relatives will not survive. Who knows, perhaps it was for a time just like this that
God allowed
you to become queenВҰ230167?вҖқ
ThenВҰ230169 EstherВҰ230170 sent this replyВҰ230171 to MordekaiВҰ230174:

вҖңGo and assemble all the Jews who live here in ShushanВҰ230184, and tell them to fast and pray for my sakeвҖ”tell them to not eat or drinkВҰ230192 anything for three days and three nightsВҰ230195. My female attendants and I will also fast in the same way. At the end of the three days, I will go in to talk to the king even though itвҖҷs against the law. I will do thatВҰ230208 even if it costs me my life.вҖқ
SoВҰ230216 MordekaiВҰ230217 wentВҰ230216 andВҰ230218 did everythingВҰ230219 that EstherВҰ230224 had toldВҰ230222 him to do.
Esther goes to the king without an invitation
/s1 EstherвҖҷs Banquet; Esther Invites the King and Haman to a Banquet; EstherвҖҷs Request to the King
Three days later, the
Queen
EstherВҰ230232 put on her royal robesВҰ230231 and
went and

stoodВҰ230234 in the innerВҰ230239 courtyardВҰ230235 of the palace, across from the kingвҖҷs house. He was in the royal palace, sittingВҰ230244 on the royal throneВҰ230247 and facing the roomвҖҷs entranceВҰ230252.
